capacity_evidence.device: one Blackhole chip from a P300c endpoint (TT_VISIBLE_DEVICES=0)
capacity_evidence.bounded_strategy: QKV/O projections, full-attention SDPA, precise sparse experts, and retained dense controls use bounded 128-token work units with consumed intermediates explicitly deallocated.
capacity_evidence.advertised_context_result: PASS: real checkpoint layer 13 returned shape [1,1,131072,2880] with finite first and last logical tokens; 32.37 s final test call.
capacity_evidence.next_failure: No device-capacity failure exists within the HF-advertised interval.
capacity_evidence.largest_all_metrics_pcc_result: Sliding/full prefill output PCC 0.992899/0.991286; following decode PCC 0.994725/0.999403; prefill K/V cache PCC at least 0.999949 and appended-token K/V PCC at least 0.999897.
capacity_evidence.diagnostic_2048: PASS after replacing the unsafe one-shot multi-head fill_cache write with bounded 128-token offset writes. The prior 0.989215 full-layer value-cache result was a placement-stride corruption, not a capacity or dtype limit.
capacity_evidence.qualification_scope: The S=131072 run is a real-weight device-capacity and finite-output qualification. Full HF-reference output/cache/following-decode PCC uses S=2048 as the largest all-metrics point because an O(S^2) CPU oracle is not constructed at the advertised endpoint.

notes: The optimized decoder preserves arbitrary positive logical prefill lengths by internal padding/chunking and permanently tests non-aligned S=3, 17, 33, 129 plus repeated decode positions 127-131. Real sliding/full S=128 decode PCC is 0.996778/0.999416; S=129 is 0.996560/0.999379. Position 127 uses a bounded sink-aware FP32 accuracy guard; positions at and above 128 use native TTNN SDPA decode. HiFi2 QKV/O projections remain selected from position 127 onward to prevent top-4 router changes at sensitive boundary positions. Persistent K/V uses BFP8_B; current-token K/V remains BF16 before cache writes.

fused_decoder_stage.capacity_change: The fused stage advanced real exercised prefill from S=17 to S=33 without changing the HF-advertised public capability.

optimized_decoder_stage.validated_decode_positions: 3 through 18, repeated 127 through 131, and 1024/2048 after matching real-weight prefill
optimized_decoder_stage.cache_storage_change: BFP8_B replaces BF16 for persistent K/V. Callers select max_cache_len up to the public context; the emitted default remains 128 for compatibility.
optimized_decoder_stage.capacity_change: The earlier S=128 reduction was based on an avoidable unchunked dense implementation. After bounding live tensors, a progressive real-weight full-layer sweep passes the HF endpoint, so current_supported_context is restored to 131072.

multichip_decoder_stage.target_hardware: four local Blackhole P300c devices in a fixed 1x4 FABRIC_1D_RING
multichip_decoder_stage.validated_decode_positions: 17 through 19, repeated 127 through 131, sequential traced 128 through 131 and 191 through 193, following-prefill positions 128/129/2048, and endpoint 131071 for both sliding and full-attention layers
multichip_decoder_stage.cache_storage_change: KV-head TP4 changes each rank from the global eight-head cache view to two local KV heads. At context 131072, each rank has 2048 physical pages and one local K or V cache contains 16,777,216 BFP8 values.
multichip_decoder_stage.page_table_contract: Replicated INT32 logical-to-physical page mappings may be arbitrary permutations. Reverse mappings cross physical and logical page boundaries in correctness, trace, and endpoint tests.
multichip_decoder_stage.trace_contract: Native short decode accepts mutable device position tensors across one captured graph. At and above position 127, sink-correct manual attention uses fixed 64-token trace banks, a device-mutable current-position tensor, and device-side causal/sliding masks. One warmed capture replays every token within a bank; recapture is required only at a 64-token page-bank boundary. Sequential reverse-page tests cover positions 128-131 and 191-193 with exact eager output and per-rank K/V writes.
multichip_decoder_stage.precision_contract: Prefill preserves the optimized baseline's layer-aware policy: sliding uses BF16-output HiFi2 active experts; full S=128 uses exact TP-local FP32 attention; full prefill uses HiFi4 active experts. Decode uses LoFi experts, HiFi2 long projections for sliding attention, and HiFi4 long projections for full attention to preserve near-tied top-4 routing at the 131072 endpoint. Expert weights remain BFP8_B and execution remains gate-selected EP4.
multichip_decoder_stage.real_weight_boundary_pcc: Across positions 127-131, attention PCC is at least 0.999397 for sliding and 0.999225 for full attention; final decode PCC remains above the accepted 0.995 threshold for both layer kinds against the current single-chip OptimizedDecoder. At S=128/129/2048, minimum prefill output PCC is 0.997231, minimum attention PCC is 0.999994, and following-decode PCC is at least 0.997504.
multichip_decoder_stage.advertised_context_result: PASS for both layer kinds: [2048,2,64,64] local K and V caches allocate on all four ranks and decode position 131071 updates reverse-mapped physical page 0 offset 63. Sliding/full final output PCC is 0.997990/0.999226 versus default-native OptimizedDecoder. Exact-manual attention/routing/final PCC is 0.999795/0.999967/0.998329 for sliding and 0.999948/0.999994/0.999280 for full, with exact top-4.
multichip_decoder_stage.capacity_change: No reduction. KV-head TP4 lowers per-device cache storage and the full 131072-token HF-advertised capability remains supported.

optimized_multichip_decoder_stage.target_hardware: four local Blackhole P300c devices in a fixed 1x4 FABRIC_1D_RING
optimized_multichip_decoder_stage.public_residual_contract: Replicated BF16 TILE DRAM [1,1,S,2880] enters and leaves every decoder layer. No gather, reshard, or all-reduce is required at the inter-layer boundary.
optimized_multichip_decoder_stage.decode_collective: Two BF16 width-sharded L1 minimal all-reduce operations per token, each using a model-owned persistent output buffer and global semaphore on the TP4 ring.
optimized_multichip_decoder_stage.persistent_l1_change_detail: Two 737280-byte BF16 minimal-all-reduce buffers (90 shards of [32,128]) plus two four-byte global semaphore words on each of 110 worker cores.
optimized_multichip_decoder_stage.persistent_dram_change_detail: Short-decode BFP8_B TP-local packed QKV and O projection copies are retained beside the BF16 prefill/long-decode weights. Positions below 127 use the BFP8_B copies; sink-correct long decode uses BF16 to preserve near-tied routing. BFP8_B tile storage is 1088 bytes per 32x32 tile.
optimized_multichip_decoder_stage.activation_sharding: Decode QKV input/output and both collective payloads use width-sharded L1. Sparse expert outputs use L1 for one-tile prefill chunks (logical S <= 32) and decode, and DRAM for larger prefill. The public residual stays replicated BF16 DRAM; sparse expert activations stay BF16 and gate-selected.
optimized_multichip_decoder_stage.prefill_expert_output_policy: The largest physically feasible direct L1 sparse-output tile is M=32. M=64 requires a 94,371,840-byte L1 allocation while only 353,472 bytes per bank remain at the allocation point, and M=128 requires 188,743,680 bytes against 1,461,504 bytes per bank. The decoder therefore selects L1 output for internally padded M<=32 and DRAM output above it.
optimized_multichip_decoder_stage.capacity_change: No reduction. Extra persistent L1/DRAM state is fixed-size and independent of max_cache_len; the 131072-token public context and per-rank paged KV allocation remain unchanged.

classification.attention_op: scaled_dot_product_attention_decode with a bounded sink-aware position-127 accuracy guard

emit_provenance.collective_collapse: Full Hugging Face weights replace emitted shards; attention and expert all-reduces plus routing mesh partition are absent from runtime.

dtype_policy.functional_baseline: BF16 weights, activations, and cache; TILE layout; DRAM by default
dtype_policy.exception: The emitted router promotes its activation and linear result to FP32 before BF16 top-k; decode heads use one workload-derived L1 height shard.
dtype_policy.optimized_policy: Hot decode uses BF16 activations/residuals/norms/router, BFP8_B attention weights below position 127, BF16 attention weights at and above position 127, BFP8_B routed expert weights with LoFi sparse matmuls, and BFP8_B persistent K/V. Short decode projections use HiFi2; long sliding/full projections preserve the selected HiFi2/HiFi4 policy. Sliding prefill uses BF16/HiFi2 sparse expert outputs; full prefill above S=128 uses bounded BF16/HiFi4 sparse chunks. Full-attention S=128 retains exact FP32 manual attention. The multichip decoder always uses EP4 gate-selected sparse experts; it has no dense all-expert execution path.
dtype_policy.deferred: Complete full-model datatype-frontier selection remains a later datatype-sweep stage.
